newsBriefing: A wide mix of headlines across the sports world highlights a day shaped by historic milestones, major roster moves and several developing controversies. In the NBA, LeBron James added yet another chapter to his legacy by surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for the most made field goals in league history, continuing a remarkable late-career run of record breaking. The NFL news cycle was dominated by personnel changes: the Buffalo Bills are reportedly acquiring wide receiver DJ Moore from the Chicago Bears in a significant trade, while Chicago is also moving on from veteran linebacker Tremaine Edmunds as part of a roster reset. The league also paused to remember longtime Packers president and team Hall of Famer Bob Harlan, who died at age 89 after decades shaping Green Bay's modern era. In hockey, Penguins star Evgeni Malkin was ejected for slashing during a heated game and could face supplemental discipline from the NHL. Off the court in basketball, free-agent guard Malik Beasley was ordered by a judge to pay $1 million to a former agency following a legal dispute. College football is also facing a developing eligibility battle as the NCAA challenges a ruling that granted Ole Miss quarterback Chambliss a sixth year of eligibility, a case that could influence future eligibility decisions across the sport. Meanwhile, global soccer made its way into the political spotlight as Lionel Messi and Inter Miami were honored at the White House following the club's recent achievements. Together, the day's headlines reflect a sports landscape where historic accomplishments, league discipline, roster shakeups and off-field decisions are all colliding at once.

picks.headline: Chet Holmgren's career night carried Oklahoma City past New York
Vegas erased a two-goal deficit and beat Detroit in overtime
Charlotte routed Boston to extend its winning streak to six games
Ten-man Newcastle beat Manchester United late, ending Michael Carrick's unbeaten start
Ville Husso made 42 saves as Anaheim routed the Islanders
Atlanta rallied from a big deficit and handed Milwaukee another lopsided loss
New Jersey rallied late and beat Toronto in a shootout, extending the Leafs' skid
Arsenal ground out a win at Brighton to widen its lead in the title race
Nottingham Forest held Manchester City to a draw, costing City more title-chase points
Joao Pedro scored a hat trick as Chelsea beat Aston Villa to strengthen its top-five push
